name: Price War
description: Chinese OEMs gain unrestricted EU access while EV demand booms — intense price competition compresses margins across the board.

defend.headline: Accelerate Epiq launch & lock fleet contracts
steps: Pull Epiq SOP forward by 6 months to establish segment presence before BYD ramps
Offer 3-year total-cost-of-ownership guarantees to fleet customers
Increase marketing spend 20% on brand trust and local service advantage
Negotiate VW Group battery volume discounts to protect per-unit margin

attack.headline: Exploit BYD's EU service and brand gap
steps: Launch 'Total Ownership' campaign emphasizing Skoda's 3,000-dealer EU network vs. BYD's 200
Offer free 5-year maintenance packages that Chinese brands can't match
Partner with leasing companies to bundle insurance + service + charging
Target BYD customers with aggressive trade-in programs after 12 months

partner.headline: Joint battery procurement across VW Group
steps: Consolidate LFP cell sourcing with VW/Audi/Porsche for maximum volume leverage
Co-invest in European gigafactory (Salzgitter) to reduce dependency on CATL/BYD
Share MEB+ platform costs across 4 brands to achieve €8k/unit battery target
Explore Northvolt or SVOLT as alternative European cell suppliers

retreat.headline: Cede sub-€20k segment, focus on €25k-35k
steps: Deprioritize Epiq if cost parity with BYD Seagull is unachievable
Reposition Skoda as 'smart value' rather than 'cheapest EV'
Redirect R&D budget from cost-down engineering to software UX differentiation
Accept 15-20% volume loss in entry segment, protect margin in core segment

name: Fortress Europe
description: Chinese OEM access is constrained while EV demand surges — a protected growth window for European OEMs to scale profitably.

defend.headline: Scale EV portfolio rapidly within protected market
steps: Maximize MEB platform output — target 500k BEV units/year by 2028
Secure long-term battery supply contracts at today's favorable pricing
Invest in charging infrastructure partnerships (Ionity, Electrify Europe)
Build out Skoda-branded home charging ecosystem

attack.headline: Capture market share from slower competitors
steps: Aggressively price Peaq to steal Hyundai Ioniq 5 and Kia EV6 customers
Launch subscription model for younger buyers (25-35) who avoid ownership
Enter commercial vehicle segment with eTransporter platform sharing
Acquire or partner with a European autonomous driving startup

partner.headline: Deepen VW Group platform advantage
steps: Advocate for SSP platform acceleration within the group
Joint investment in Cariad software reboot with Rivian tech
Collaborate with Seat/Cupra on shared manufacturing for Southern Europe
Explore white-label battery supply to smaller EU OEMs for revenue diversification

retreat.headline: No retreat needed — invest for growth
steps: This is the most favorable scenario — retreat is not recommended
Instead, use windfall margin to build strategic reserves
Pre-invest in solid-state battery R&D for Horizon 3
Build brand equity that survives when tariffs eventually come down

name: Slow Burn
description: Chinese competition is blocked but EV demand stalls — compliance costs mount while ICE revenue declines slowly. A war of attrition.

defend.headline: Optimize ICE profitability while managing compliance
steps: Maximize contribution margin on remaining ICE/PHEV models
Lobby for Euro 7 timeline extensions through ACEA industry coalition
Reduce BEV capex burn rate — stretch Epiq launch if demand doesn't justify it
Implement aggressive cost-down programs across non-EV operations

attack.headline: Use compliance as competitive weapon
steps: Achieve Euro 7 compliance faster than Dacia/Renault to gain temporary monopoly
Market Skoda as 'the responsible choice' to ESG-conscious fleet managers
Poach Dacia customers who lose access to non-compliant models
Build CSMS cyber compliance as a consulting service for smaller OEMs

partner.headline: Share compliance burden with VW Group
steps: Pool CO2 credits across VW Group brands to minimize penalty exposure
Joint R&D on Euro 7 catalytic systems with Audi/Porsche
Shared CSMS platform with Seat to halve per-brand implementation cost
Explore regulatory sandbox partnerships with EU transport ministries

retreat.headline: Exit lowest-margin ICE segments early
steps: Discontinue ICE variants that can't achieve Euro 7 compliance profitably
Consolidate production to 2-3 key plants, close underutilized facilities
Redirect freed-up capital to BEV ramp when demand recovers
Accept 10% volume decline to protect group-level margin targets

name: Perfect Storm
description: The worst case: Chinese competition floods the EU while EV demand stalls. Price pressure from above, compliance costs from below.

defend.headline: Survival mode — protect core revenue at all costs
steps: Freeze all discretionary capex; redirect to cash preservation
Lock existing fleet contracts with multi-year extensions at reduced margin
Accelerate cost-down programs: target 15% reduction in fixed costs
Negotiate emergency supply chain flexibility with Tier 1 suppliers

attack.headline: Counter-position on trust and residual value
steps: Launch guaranteed residual value program that Chinese brands can't offer
Emphasize data privacy and cybersecurity vs. Chinese-connected vehicles
Target fleet managers with 'total cost of disruption' analysis
Build referral programs leveraging Skoda's existing 8M+ customer base

partner.headline: Consolidate within VW Group for survival
steps: Merge Skoda and Seat operations to eliminate duplicate overhead
Petition EU for emergency anti-dumping tariffs on Chinese EVs
Form industry consortium with Stellantis/Renault for shared compliance costs
Explore strategic investment from a non-automotive tech partner

retreat.headline: Strategic contraction to defensible segments
steps: Exit all segments below €25k — cede to Chinese price leaders
Close 2 of 5 manufacturing plants; consolidate to Mladá Boleslav + Kvasiny
Reduce model lineup from 10 to 6 core vehicles
Redeploy freed capital to India/ASEAN where Skoda has growth runway
